---
title: "Phase 2 — skycell / SCA overlap"
subtitle: "Finding depth-6 skycells for each selected pair and the exact (pointing, SCA) list to simulate"
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
from pathlib import Path
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from astropy.table import Table

REPO = Path("../..").resolve()

POINTINGS = Table.read(REPO / "catalogs/detection/selected_pointings.ecsv",
                       format="ascii.ecsv").to_pandas().sort_values(
    ["PAIR_ID", "PASS", "EXPOSURE"]).reset_index(drop=True)
SCA_TABLE = Table.read(REPO / "catalogs/detection/skycell_overlap_sca.ecsv",
                        format="ascii.ecsv").to_pandas()
PT_TABLE = Table.read(REPO / "catalogs/detection/skycell_overlap_pointing.ecsv",
                      format="ascii.ecsv").to_pandas()
SELECTED = Table.read(REPO / "catalogs/detection/selected_skycells.ecsv",
                      format="ascii.ecsv").to_pandas()
NEED_SIM = Table.read(REPO / "catalogs/detection/pointings_sca_to_simulate.ecsv",
                      format="ascii.ecsv").to_pandas()
```

## What Phase 2 does

Phase 2 is purely geometric — no image data is simulated. For each of
the 30 pointings from [Phase 1](phase1_pointings.qmd), we:

1. Build the 18-SCA WCS at the pointing (via
   `galsim.roman.getWCS(world_pos, SCAs=None, date, PA)`), which
   applies the SIAF field-of-view offsets and the Roman distortion
   model to produce a GalSim `CelestialWCS` per SCA.
2. Extract the four corner positions of each 4088×4088 SCA.
3. Call `romancal.skycell.match.find_skycell_matches(corners)` to get
   the list of skycells the SCA footprint overlaps — this walks the
   skymap KDTree and tests polygon overlap against each candidate
   projection region's skycell grid.

From the 540 `(pointing, SCA) → skycell(s)` triples we build two
aggregate tables and pick one depth-6 skycell per pair for Phase 3/4.

## Overlap counts

Every pair has ≥ 1 skycell where all 6 pointings overlap
(**depth 6**, the design maximum); depth falls off near the edges of the
pair's combined footprint.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "Per-pair skycell-depth distribution. A depth-d skycell gets contributions from d of the pair's 6 pointings."
depth_table = (PT_TABLE
               .groupby(["PAIR_ID", "n_pointings"]).size()
               .rename("n_skycells").reset_index()
               .pivot(index="PAIR_ID", columns="n_pointings",
                      values="n_skycells").fillna(0).astype(int))
depth_table.columns = [f"depth {c}" for c in depth_table.columns]
depth_table
```

## Per-pair coverage maps

Each panel shows one pair. Coloured polygons are skycells in the
(pair, skycell) table — fill colour encodes mosaic depth (0 → max).
Thin outlines are the 18 SCA footprints of each of the pair's 6
pointings, colour-coded by pointing. The red thick outline is the
Phase-2-selected skycell for that pair (the deepest one nearest the
pair's nominal centre). The black `+` is the pair centre.

```{python}
#| echo: false
from IPython.display import Image, display
for pid in sorted(SELECTED["PAIR_ID"].unique()):
    path = REPO / f"output/detection/phase2/coverage_pair_{pid}.png"
    if path.exists():
        display(Image(filename=str(path)))
```

## The selected skycells

One depth-6 skycell per pair — selected to be the deepest skycell
nearest the pair's nominal centre.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "The five selected skycells, one per pair. `n_pointings` is the mosaic depth (design maximum = 6); `n_sca_events` is the total number of distinct (pointing, SCA) contributions (may exceed 6 when the skycell straddles an SCA boundary inside a single pointing)."
SELECTED[["PAIR_ID", "skycell_idx", "skycell_name",
          "skycell_ra", "skycell_dec", "n_pointings", "n_sca_events"]].style.format(
    {"skycell_ra": "{:.3f}", "skycell_dec": "{:.3f}"})
```

## L2 files to simulate

For each selected skycell, the set of `(pointing, SCA)` pairs whose
footprint overlaps the skycell:

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "L2 file count per pair (distinct `(POINTING_IDX, SCA)` combinations after dedup)."
l2_counts = (NEED_SIM
             .drop_duplicates(["PAIR_ID", "POINTING_IDX", "SCA"])
             .groupby("PAIR_ID").size().reset_index(name="n_l2_files"))
l2_counts["n_pointings"] = NEED_SIM.groupby("PAIR_ID")["POINTING_IDX"].nunique().values
l2_counts["scas_per_pointing_mean"] = (l2_counts["n_l2_files"] /
                                       l2_counts["n_pointings"]).round(2)
l2_counts
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "The full per-skycell list of L2 files Phase 4 will produce (one row per `(pointing, SCA)` pair per selected skycell)."
NEED_SIM[["PAIR_ID", "skycell_idx", "skycell_name",
          "POINTING_IDX", "PASS", "VISIT", "EXPOSURE",
          "SCA"]].sort_values(
    ["PAIR_ID", "POINTING_IDX", "SCA"]).reset_index(drop=True)
```

## Self-checks

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "End-of-phase assertions on the overlap output."
checks = [
    ("5 skycells selected (one per pair)",        len(SELECTED) == 5),
    ("every selected skycell has depth 6",
     bool((SELECTED["n_pointings"] == 6).all())),
    ("every pair has exactly one selected skycell",
     SELECTED["PAIR_ID"].nunique() == SELECTED.shape[0]),
    ("every pointing appears in the SCA table",
     SCA_TABLE["POINTING_IDX"].nunique() == len(POINTINGS)),
    ("all selected skycells have ≥ 6 distinct L2 contributions",
     bool((SELECTED["n_sca_events"] >= 6).all())),
    ("n_sca_events ≥ n_pointings for every (pair, skycell)",
     bool((PT_TABLE["n_sca_events"] >= PT_TABLE["n_pointings"]).all())),
]
pd.DataFrame(checks, columns=["check", "pass"])
```

## Reproducing

```bash
pixi run python scripts/detection/02_skycell_overlap.py
```

Writes:

- `catalogs/detection/skycell_overlap_sca.ecsv` — every
  `(pointing, SCA, skycell)` triple
- `catalogs/detection/skycell_overlap_pointing.ecsv` — aggregated to
  one row per `(pair, skycell)`, with depth and contributing
  pointings/SCAs
- `catalogs/detection/selected_skycells.ecsv` — one depth-6 skycell
  per pair
- `catalogs/detection/pointings_sca_to_simulate.ecsv` — the
  `(pointing, SCA)` list Phase 4 uses
- `output/detection/phase2/coverage_pair_*.png`

## Checkpoint

Phase 2 deliverable. Before Phase 3, we need to pick a storage
location with the user for the L2/L3 binary products (they won't live
in the repo). Default: `output/detection/{l2,l3}/` in-tree, with the
corresponding subpaths added to `.gitignore`.
